type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding debug/debug_logger params to StreamListener.__init__, guarded DEBUG-level trace logs in receive() (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush) and in flush()/reset block, plus small truncation helper. Purely additive (new params + logging calls), no changes to existing detection logic.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Add idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking in receive(), and new tick(now_ts=None) method to StreamListener that emits StreamResponse(..., chunk='') on timeout and resets listener state unconditionally (respecting allow_reuse for reuse afterward).

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I'll own this file since my debug logging touches nearly every branch of receive()/flush()/reset. I'll implement your idle_timeout_s + tick() feature per your SPEC too. Please send SPEC with exact signature for tick(), where self._last_chunk_ts should be updated in receive(), and exact reset behavior/interaction with allow_reuse.

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I'll own this file since my feature adds a new tick() method plus a second state-reset path (timeout-triggered) that needs to stay consistent with the existing reuse reset in receive(). Please send a SPEC for your debug logging additions (params, log call sites, format) and I'll fold them in without touching your logic.

type: DEFER
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
spec: Add to StreamListener.__init__ signature: idle_timeout_s: float | None = None (new kwarg, after allow_reuse). In __init__ body set: self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s; self._last_chunk_ts = None.

In receive(...): whenever a chunk is actually processed as a real, non-empty chunk update — i.e., right after the existing 'if chunk_message is None: return' / exception guard confirms chunk_message is a non-empty string (so at the top of receive, after the try/except that extracts chunk_message and before the cache_hit/start/end logic, but only when chunk_message is truthy) — set self._last_chunk_ts = now_ts, where now_ts should be obtained via time.time() (import time at top of file) called once at the top of receive() and stored in a local var, then assigned to self._last_chunk_ts. Do NOT update _last_chunk_ts for None/empty chunk_message. This must be the ONLY change to receive()'s existing control flow — do not alter detection logic.

Add a new method on StreamListener:

    def tick(self, now_ts: float | None = None) -> StreamResponse | None:
        """Check for idle timeout and emit a terminal empty-chunk StreamResponse if exceeded."""

Behavior:
1. If self.idle_timeout_s is None: return None immediately (feature disabled).
2. now_ts = now_ts if now_ts is not None else time.time()
3. Only fires if the stream has started but not yet ended: condition is . If that's False, return None.
4. If self._last_chunk_ts is None, return None (no chunk received yet to measure elapsed time from — avoids false positive before first chunk arrives, though stream_start=True implies at least one chunk was received in practice).
5. elapsed = now_ts - self._last_chunk_ts. If elapsed < self.idle_timeout_s: return None (not timed out yet).
6. Timeout case: build . (Note: a separate feature may add an  field to StreamResponse later — if that field exists when you implement this, set it to True on this response; otherwise omit it, do not add the field yourself.)
7. Reset state UNCONDITIONALLY (regardless of allow_reuse) so the listener is never left stuck in a started state: self.stream_start = False; self.stream_end = False; self.cache_hit = False; self.field_start_queue = []; self.field_end_queue = Queue(); self._last_chunk_ts = None. (Same fields as the existing reuse-reset block in receive(), but always applied here — allow_reuse only controls whether a listener that has *already* naturally ended (stream_end=True) can be reused on the next receive() call; it does not gate this timeout reset.)
8. Return .

tick() is intended to be called periodically (e.g. every N ms) by the streaming driver/system, not from within receive(). It must not raise if idle_timeout_s is None or if no stream has started — those are normal no-op cases.

If an on_chunk-style callback is ever added to this class, tick()'s timeout response should be routed through it the same way normal chunks are, but no such callback exists in the current codebase, so no action needed unless you're already adding one.
note: tick() and _last_chunk_ts tracking for idle-timeout feature

type: DEFER
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
spec: Add opt-in debug tracing to StreamListener. Implement exactly as follows so tests pass on exact string match:

1. __init__ signature: add two new params at the end:
   debug: bool = False, debug_logger: "logging.Logger | None" = None
   Body: self.debug = debug; self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger("dspy.streaming.listener")
   Add `import logging` at top of file.

2. Add a small truncation helper (module-level or static method), e.g.:
   def _truncate_preview(text: str, limit: int = 80) -> str:
       if text is None:
           return ""
       if len(text) <= limit:
           return text
       return text[:limit] + "..."
   Must NOT append "..." when len(text) <= limit.

3. Guard every debug log with: if self.debug and self._logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): ...
   All at logging.DEBUG level via self._logger.debug(...).

4. Trace point - start detection: in receive(), right after `self.stream_start = True` is set inside the
   `if start_identifier in concat_message:` branch (the branch that computes `chunk_message = concat_message[value_start_index:].lstrip()` etc.), after that block finishes computing chunk_message, log:
     self._logger.debug(
         f"Start detection: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', "
         f"stream_start=True, buffer_preview='{_truncate_preview(chunk_message)}'"
     )
   Example outputs: "Start detection: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', stream_start=True, buffer_preview='\n'" and "Start detection: adapter=JSONAdapter, field='answer', stream_start=True, buffer_preview='Hello world!'"

5. Trace point - rolling end check: in the `if self.stream_start:` block, when `re.search(end_identifier, concat_message)` matches (the branch that sets self.stream_end = True and calls self.flush()), BEFORE or right at that detection, log:
     self._logger.debug(
         f"Rolling end check: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', "
         f"reason='regex_match', buffered_size={len(concat_message)}"
     )
   Example: "Rolling end check: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', reason='regex_match', buffered_size=25"

6. Trace point - emit chunk: right before `return StreamResponse(self.predict_name, self.signature_field_name, token)` at the end of receive(), log:
     is_last_chunk = self.stream_end  (i.e. True when this is the flush-triggered final emission, False otherwise)
     self._logger.debug(
         f"Emit chunk: len(token)={len(token)}, queue_size={self.field_end_queue.qsize()}, is_last_chunk={is_last_chunk}"
     )
   Example: "Emit chunk: len(token)=12, queue_size=3, is_last_chunk=False"

7. Trace point - flush: in flush(), before returning (log right before each return statement, or restructure to a single exit log covering the truncated buffer length actually returned), log:
     adapter_name = settings.adapter.__class__.__name__ if settings.adapter else "ChatAdapter"
     self._logger.debug(
         f"Flush: adapter={adapter_name}, field='{self.signature_field_name}', "
         f"truncated_buffer_length={boundary_index}"
     )
   truncated_buffer_length = boundary_index used to slice last_tokens[:boundary_index] in that branch (i.e. length of the returned string). Example: "Flush: adapter=ChatAdapter, field='answer', truncated_buffer_length=15"

8. Trace point - reset: in receive(), inside `if self.stream_end: if self.allow_reuse: ...` block, after clearing state (self.stream_end/self.cache_hit/self.field_start_queue/self.field_end_queue/self.stream_start reset), log:
     self._logger.debug(f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)")
   Example: "State reset for field 'answer' (allow_reuse=True)"

9. All f-string content must match example format literally: no extra spaces, same quote style (single quotes around field name and buffer_preview values), same key=value formatting (no spaces around '=').

10. Do not change any detection/flush algorithm behavior or StreamResponse schema — logging only, purely additive, zero behavior change when debug=False.

If your tick()/timeout reset also clears state (a "second reset path"), please also emit the same-style log there: f"State reset for field '{self.signature_field_name}' (allow_reuse=True)" only guarded by self.debug similarly, if and only if that reset path also honors allow_reuse semantics equivalently to the existing one. If it's a different scenario (unconditional reset regardless of allow_reuse), it's fine to skip a log there or use your own reasonable message — this is not tested, just noted for consistency; not a hard requirement.

Please ACK by echoing this SPEC understanding in a CLAIM reply.

type: CLAIM

I have your full tick()/idle_timeout_s SPEC and will implement it plus my own debug-logging feature in this file. Sending DONE when complete.

type: DEFER
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
spec: Add to StreamListener.__init__ signature: idle_timeout_s: float | None = None (new kwarg, after allow_reuse). In __init__ body set: self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s and self._last_chunk_ts = None.

In receive(...): update self._last_chunk_ts to the current time whenever a real, non-empty chunk_message is processed. Specifically, right after the existing try/except block that extracts chunk_message and confirms it is not None (so once we know chunk_message is a non-empty string), set self._last_chunk_ts = now_ts, where now_ts is obtained via time.time() (import time at top of file), computed once near the top of receive() in a local variable. Do NOT update _last_chunk_ts when chunk_message is None/empty. This should be the only change to receive()'s existing control flow -- do not alter detection logic.

Add a new method on StreamListener named tick, with signature: def tick(self, now_ts: float | None = None) -> StreamResponse | None. Docstring: Check for idle timeout and emit a terminal empty-chunk StreamResponse if exceeded.

Behavior:
1. If self.idle_timeout_s is None, return None immediately (feature disabled).
2. Set now_ts = now_ts if not None else time.time().
3. Only proceed if the stream has started but not yet ended, i.e. self.stream_start is True and self.stream_end is False. Otherwise return None.
4. If self._last_chunk_ts is None, return None (no chunk received yet to measure elapsed time from).
5. Compute elapsed = now_ts - self._last_chunk_ts. If elapsed < self.idle_timeout_s, return None (not timed out yet).
6. Timeout case: build response = StreamResponse(self.predict_name, self.signature_field_name, "") -- note the chunk field is an empty string. If a separate feature has added an is_last_chunk field to StreamResponse by the time you implement this, set it to True on this response; otherwise do not add that field yourself.
7. Reset listener state UNCONDITIONALLY (regardless of allow_reuse) so the listener is never left stuck in a started state: set self.stream_start = False, self.stream_end = False, self.cache_hit = False, self.field_start_queue = [], self.field_end_queue = Queue(), self._last_chunk_ts = None. This mirrors the existing reuse-reset block in receive(), but is always applied here -- allow_reuse only controls whether a listener that has already naturally ended (stream_end True) can be reused on the next receive() call; it does not gate this timeout reset.
8. Return response.

tick() is intended to be called periodically (e.g. every N ms) by the streaming driver/system, not from within receive(). It must not raise if idle_timeout_s is None or if no stream has started -- those are normal no-op cases.

If an on_chunk-style callback is ever added to this class, tick's timeout response should be routed through it the same way normal chunks are, but no such callback exists in the current codebase, so no action needed unless you are already adding one.
note: tick() and _last_chunk_ts tracking for idle-timeout feature; ignore my previous DEFER message if it looked garbled (shell backtick issue), this is the clean version
